opp.name: DWTC _ iRisk & iContract _2025
opp.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP (Zyad) & VP (Jason) - current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (all of their conferences & exhibitions for near future are cancelled/postponed) - have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins and strengthen economics now and to prepare for the reopening and rescheduling of the backlog), seemed to resonate with VP who will discuss with upper mgt, (I offered to present to them and/or bring Carl in in helpful). 11th May- Regional unrest slowing down projects at present- KIV and keep pushing to accelerate. 17th April - Response from Proj Lead: "T hanks for checking in Dan. Things are slowly but surely improving however the event/exhibition confidence and surge like previous months will take some time before our calendar is reset with firm dates/expected foot fall. Budgetary controls are still in place so shall go with the flow for now till we fully rebound". Will move back to Q2 and monitor. 19th Mar - The war has forced them to reassess priorities, the project is paused for now, but (at this stage) still expecting to restart in Q1 (their Q2) 3rd Mar - Nudged Zyad for an update, session to evaluate options is now mid-March, will synch with him asap afterwards. 23rd Feb - Submitted revised scope budgetary estimate pricing ahead of management meeting end Feb. 12th Feb - Spoke to Zyad, unfortunately scope has reduced back to CLM + iSupplier repository only at this phase (though interested in other modules later) - likely to be too small in scope now or min pricing will make us uncompetitive, will get the pricing from Raj & discuss with Carl - reducing confidence to 5%, 10th Oct - Budgetary Estimate provided - next steps formal RFP once budget confirmed 7th Oct - Requested Budgetary Estimate, also AVP will be visiting us in DPW Amsterdam 11th Sept - Zyad attended the Dubai World Tour (AVP also invited), positive conversation, but will be a formal process. Aug 26th - Courtesy reach-out to Sami to see if we can be of any assistance in the process. July 30th - Intro call with Samy - focus is on CLM, just doing initial research prior to gaining management approval for a formal RFP.
opp.ais_why:
• Across 1,709 chars of NextStep covering eight dated entries from Jul 2025 to May 2026 ‚Äî including disco call, budgetary estimate, scope reduction and war-related delays ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, or GenAI. The 12 Feb 2026 entry: 'Spoke to Zyad, unfortunately scope has reduced back to CLM + iSupplier repository only at this phase (though interested in other modules later) - likely to be too small in scope now or min pricing will make us uncompetitive, will get the pricing from Raj.' Scope is shrinking not expanding into AI.
• On 17 Apr 2026 Project Lead Zyad responded: 'event/exhibition confidence and surge like previous months will take some time before our calendar is reset with firm dates/expected foot fall. Budgetary controls are still in place so shall go with the flow for now.' On 11 May 2026: 'Regional unrest slowing down projects at present.' The deal is paused with shrunk scope; Merlin is bundled in Products but never named in any rep-buyer dialogue.
• Confidence is Medium. Scope contraction to 'CLM + iSupplier repository only' eliminates whatever AI positioning might have existed, and Zyad's communications are all budget/timing-focused rather than capability-driven. Held at AI Resistant given absence of AI keyword and shrinking deal footprint.

contacts.name: Zyad Khan
contacts.title: Associate Director - Procurement, Contracts and ESG

contacts.name: Farzan Almarzooqi

items.quote: I report into the senior vice president of IT

items.area: Quantified ROI / 3-year TCO not yet built
items.quote: I would want to present a 3 year TCO model around it

items.area: No restart date for paused evaluation
items.quote: shall go with the flow for now till we fully rebound

metrics.narrative: The business problem is qualitative and audit-driven, not yet quantified. On the 11 Feb 2026 call Zyad Khan said audit 'have been mocking this contract management or dashboard as something obsolete' and that he wants 'a 3 year TCO model around it, not just for the ROI sake because I'm also building a case with audit'. He also flagged a concrete cost lever: phasing out '5,000 envelopes from DocuSign' to free budget via a Certinal/eSignature module. No hard savings, cycle-time, or headcount numbers have been stated, so the value case remains directional; building the quantified 3-year TCO is the outstanding move.

champion.narrative: Zyad Khan (Associate Director - Procurement, Contracts and ESG; Salesforce Evaluator) is a developing champion. Evidence of advocacy: he ruled out the audit-suggested in-house build in Zycus's favour, framed a modular 'start small and scale up' adoption, planned a 3-year TCO model, and has direct access to economic authority (he reports to the SVP of IT and appeared jointly with Jason Tranter on 18 May 2026). What is still missing: he has not yet presented to the management committee or secured upper-management escalation, and the promised TCO model is not finalized. His engagement is active but paused by the spend freeze (last contact 18 May 2026), not cooling.

competition.narrative: This is effectively a sole-source pursuit with no active peer-vendor bake-off. The audit-recommended in-house build was explicitly declined by Zyad on 11 Feb 2026 in Zycus's favour. The named Salesforce competitors (iCertis, Sirion Labs, Ironclad, Agiloft) have never been raised by the buyer on any call or in Next_Step__c and read as dormant CRM placeholders. The real current threat is do-nothing / status quo: the conflict-driven budget freeze has indefinitely delayed the decision. We win by keeping the CLM framed as audit-risk mitigation and margin protection during the downturn, plus DocuSign consolidation as immediate ROI.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific and buyer-articulated: audit is 'mocking this contract management or dashboard as something obsolete' and recommended an in-house build, which Zyad Khan rejected to avoid diverting the IT team from revenue work (11 Feb 2026). DWTC lacks a modern CLM for a very diverse, multi-event supply chain ('3 different contracts, 3 different supply chains'), wants renewal/expiry management, and is paying for ~5,000 DocuSign envelopes it wants to consolidate. Pain owner is Zyad Khan (Procurement/Contracts), with audit as the internal pressure source. The overriding blocker is external: the conflict has frozen non-essential spend.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 10 Oct 2025 ('formal RFP once budget confirmed')
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal/InfoSec review, procurement channel) have not been reached: the deal never progressed past budgetary pricing and stalled before an RFP. The known forcing functions are commercial, not legal: an unbudgeted-spend approval must clear the quarterly management committee, and DocuSign envelope renewal is a budget lever Zyad wants to redirect. Visibility here will only come once budget reopens and a formal RFP/scope is confirmed; asking the buyer for the intended procurement and approval steps at restart would create it.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits above Zyad Khan: on 11 Feb 2026 he said 'I report into the senior vice president of IT' and that unbudgeted spend 'has to go through an unbudgeted approval' via a quarterly management committee. Jason Tranter (AVP Procurement and Contracts, Salesforce Decision Maker) is the most senior mapped buyer and committed on 18 May 2026 to 'discuss with upper mgt' the margin-protection case. The true economic buyer (the SVP of IT) is named by role but not yet mapped by name or engaged directly; sponsorship today is passive and routed through the management committee.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence: a July 2025 intro with Samyukta, a 30 Sep 2025 group demo drawing in Jason Tranter (AVP), Farzan Almarzooqi (IT Director) and Yamen Hmidan, then a 11 Feb 2026 commercial-scoping call with the relevant stakeholder path was budgetary estimate -> revised scope pricing -> internal management-committee session (targeted 27 Feb) -> intended formal RFP once budget confirmed. That process froze on 19 Mar 2026 when the regional conflict forced a spend pause; the approval chain runs Zyad -> Jason -> SVP of IT / quarterly management committee. No RFP has been issued.

sources: Avoma call 30 Sep 2025 (Jason Tranter, Zyad Khan, Samyukta Rajendran)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria emerged from the 30 Sep 2025 demo and 11 Feb 2026 call: off-the-shelf modular CLM (authoring plus repository), Merlin intake for PR screening, renewal/expiry management, integration with the relevant stakeholder and eSignature, and phased 'piecemeal' expansion. Jason Tranter probed AI maturity and case studies ('are there case studies on it') and integration back into IT systems, while Zyad valued the renewal-management repository and DocuSign phase-out. Formal evaluation criteria (weighted RFP scorecard) are not documented because a formal RFP was deferred pending budget approval.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS field is 3.0 / Negative: eight dated NextStep entries from Jul 2025 to May 2026 carry zero AI/Merlin/agentic mention and scope shrank to CLM + iSupplier repository. Calls confirm this: the 30 Sep 2025 Merlin intake demo drew polite interest from Jason but no follow-through, and the commercial 11 Feb 2026 call was entirely about price/scope, not AI.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: DUBAI WORLD TRADE CENTRE — Formal Evaluation · $110K · closes 31 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 29 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 52.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 13 is anchored on the stage win (29), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field is 3.0 / Negative: eight dated NextStep entries from Jul 2025 to May 2026 carry zero AI/Merlin/agentic mention and scope shrank to CLM + iSupplier repository. confidence_signals.summary: Buyer remains reachable and constructive (inbound reply 18 May 2026), but there has been no transcribed buyer call since 11 Feb 2026 and the decision is frozen on an external blocker; engagement is cooling on timing, not on interest.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 8 Aug 2025 (~330 days old). In Formal Evaluation the whole time (no stage-date submission fields populated). Close date 31 Dec 2026 is 181 days out, pushed there from 30 Jul 2026 on 8 Jun 2026. Last buyer inbound 18 May 2026 (46 days ago); last rep touch 25 May 2026. Paused since 19 Mar 2026 (~106 days) on regional conflict. Owner reassigned Dan Quinn -> Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun 2026. Light 2-module scope means 4-5 months from restart to close is feasible, so the date holds IF budget reopens by early Q4.
evidence: Deal paused 19 Mar 2026 (Next_Step__c: 'The war has forced them to reassess priorities, the project is paused for now, but still expecting to restart in Q1 (their Q2)').

18 May 2026: met Zyad Khan (HOP) and Jason Tranter (AVP) - 'all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues'; Jason will discuss a margin-protection investment case with upper management.
17 Apr 2026: Zyad Khan - 'Budgetary controls are still in place so shall go with the flow for now till we fully rebound.'
12 Feb 2026: scope reduced back to CLM + iSupplier repository only, 'likely to be too small in scope now or min pricing will make us uncompetitive'.
Buyer not disengaged: constructive inbound reply 18 May 2026, tone positive ('seemed to resonate with VP').
Pipeline forecast is honest; close date carries timing risk tied to conflict resolution and budget reopening - recommend Omitted until a restart date is set.
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal paused by force majeure: regional conflict/blockade has frozen all non-essential DWTC spend and cancelled/postponed their event calendar. Buyer stays constructively engaged (last inbound 18 May 2026, 46 days ago) and signals intent to restart 'later in the year', but no restart date is set and scope has shrunk to CLM + iSupplier repository. Close date 31 Dec 2026 is credible only if budget reopens by Q3/Q4 2026.

flags: Map the economic buyer by name: Zyad reports to the SVP of IT who owns unbudgeted approval, yet only Jason (AVP) is mapped; get a name and a route to that seat before restart.
Keep the case defensive, not discretionary: package audit-risk mitigation, margin protection, and DocuSign consolidation savings so CLM survives the spend freeze as a cost-saver.
Pre-clear Oracle EBS integration with Farzan Almarzooqi now so IT sign-off does not add weeks once budget reopens.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is externally stalled, not lost; the win levers are re-timing to budget reopening, mapping true budget authority, and keeping the value case defensive.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the sole vendor under active consideration; there is no live peer-vendor bake-off. The strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / status quo, driven by the conflict-induced budget freeze that has paused all non-essential spend since 19 Mar 2026. The audit-recommended in-house build was declined by Zyad Khan on 11 Feb 2026 in Zycus's favour. The Salesforce-listed peers (iCertis, Sirion Labs, Ironclad, Agiloft) have never been named by the buyer and remain dormant placeholders; no re-ranking this run as no new buyer call surfaced any of them.
